question_text: In the figure below, PT is a tangent to the circle with centre O at the point of contact T. If OT = 5 cm and OP = 13 cm, find the length of PT.

answer_key.explanation: OT is perpendicular to PT at the point of contact, so PT² = OP² − OT² = 169 − 25 = 144, giving PT = 12 cm.
answer_key.related_concept: Tangent perpendicular to radius at point of contact

visual.caption: Circle with external point and tangent.
visual.altText: Circle centred at O with radius five units. A tangent line from external point P touches the circle at T, and the radius O T is drawn. The right angle between O T and P T is shown by a small square at T.
